meta.tagline: What current immigration & guest-worker policy costs you — counted from the government's own numbers.
meta.note: Figures are official periodic agency releases, not real-time feeds. Each item shows its source and the date it covers.

metrics.label: H-1B registrations filed
metrics.sub: ~135,000 were selected (≈29%). Statutory cap: 85,000 new per year.

metrics.label: H-1B petitions approved
metrics.sub: ~142,000 were NEW employment; the rest were renewals.
metrics.source: USCIS FY2024 H-1B Report to Congress

metrics.source: DOL OFLC H-2B Statistical Factsheet FY2024

metrics.label: Remittances sent abroad from the U.S.
metrics.sub: The U.S. is the world's #1 remittance-sending country.

metrics.sub: Mexico is the world's #2 remittance recipient.
metrics.updateCadence: Annual — AUTO via World Bank API

metrics.label: Projected outflow accruing this year
metrics.sub: Live projection at the 2024 annual rate — NOT a measured real-time figure. Illustrates scale only.

metrics.group: Contested figures — shown as a transparent range
metrics.label: Unauthorized population

metrics.group: Contested figures — shown as a transparent range
metrics.label: Net fiscal impact of illegal immigration

estimates.detail: Gross cost minus ~$32B in taxes paid by this group.

estimates.finding: ~$80K lifetime net COST per unlawful immigrant
estimates.detail: Vs. roughly +$350K net contribution per average LEGAL immigrant (30-yr NPV, 2025 update).
Finds immigrants reduced deficits; published a direct rebuttal calling FAIR's method flawed.

metrics.sub: There is no single agreed number — credible institutions land from large net cost (FAIR) to net benefit (Cato), with AEI in between distinguishing legal vs. unlawful immigrants. We show all three and link each so you can judge. Displaying the full spread is what keeps this site credible and fact-check-proof.
metrics.source: FAIR · AEI · Cato (see each link above)
metrics.updateCadence: ~Annual — manual, human-reviewed; watcher flags new studies
